lead: A moving day in Saxmundham comes with its own small considerations: this is an east-Suffolk market town set in gentle countryside, a fair run out from Ipswich, with a high street and older streets that reward a little planning. As a family firm based in Westerfield, near Ipswich, we know the roads out to IP17 well and have carried many homes into the town. This checklist walks you through the run-up, the morning and your first hours in the new home, so your Saxmundham move stays calm rather than rushed.

sections.h: Two Weeks Out: Plan for the Distance and Access
sections.body:
With a fortnight to go, two things deserve early thought for a Saxmundham move: the distance and the access. Saxmundham is a good run out into east Suffolk, so timings on the day matter more than they might for a move within Ipswich, and it is worth confirming everything with us early so we can plan the route and the schedule properly. At the same time, picture where a removal lorry can stop at both ends. If your new home is on or near the high street, or on one of the older streets, some can be narrow, so let us know and we will plan accordingly, perhaps using a smaller vehicle to shuttle from a larger one parked nearby.

This is also the fortnight to confirm your moving date, start running down the freezer, notify the important people of your change of address and arrange mail redirection. If your chain is looking uncertain, talk to us now about storage rather than leaving it late. Gather your packing materials this week too, or ask us to supply them: good boxes, plenty of tape, bubble wrap for the fragile things and marker pens for labelling. Having everything in the house before you begin means the packing flows steadily rather than stalling halfway through.

A week out, packing should be well underway, and the key is to work room by room rather than dipping in and out everywhere. Start with the spaces you use least: the spare room, the loft, the garage and the shed. The loft is the classic trap in any house, holding far more than anyone remembers, and finding heavy boxes up a ladder on moving morning throws the whole timetable out, which matters all the more when you have a longer drive to Saxmundham ahead of you. Clear those awkward spaces this week while you have the time.

Label every box clearly on the side as well as the top, marking the room it belongs to in your new Saxmundham home along with a note of the contents. As we unload, we carry each box straight to the right room, so good labels stop everything piling up in the hall. Colour coding works beautifully: a colour per room, matching stickers on the boxes and a matching sheet on each door at the new house, so the crew can unload at speed even when seeing the property for the first time, which keeps a long moving day on schedule.

Set aside anything precious, important documents, jewellery and small valuables, to travel in your own car rather than the lorry. Mark fragile boxes on more than one side, and keep a simple numbered inventory if any part of your move involves storage or a staggered handover. By the end of this week, the only things still to pack should be the kettle, the breakfast things and the beds you are sleeping in the night before.

The day before your move is for finishing everything off, and with a longer journey to Saxmundham on the cards, being genuinely ready the night before is more important than ever. Defrost and empty the fridge and freezer, complete the last of the packing, and strip all but tonight's beds, so that when we arrive in the morning the house is sealed and ready apart from the kettle and the breakfast things. Unfinished packing when the crew turns up is the single biggest cause of a slow day, and a slow start eats into the time you need for the drive and the unload.

Pack your essentials box, the most important box of all, and keep it for your own car rather than the lorry. Into it goes the kettle, mugs, tea, milk and biscuits, phone chargers, basic toiletries, a change of clothes, any medication, important documents and a few toys for the children. This is the box that keeps everyone going and lets you make a brew the moment you arrive in Saxmundham without hunting through the van. Take and note your meter readings at the old property, and charge your phone overnight.

Think through the morning too. An early start helps on a move out to east Suffolk, giving plenty of time for the drive and a relaxed unload at the other end rather than a race against the light in winter. If your new home is near the high street or on a narrow street, picture where the lorry will sit. Clear a path from the front door to the kerb at both ends, and if you have children or pets, arrange care for the day or set up one cleared room as their safe base.

sections.h: Moving Morning: An Early, Steady Start
sections.body:
On the morning itself, being ready and being present makes all the difference, and an early, steady start sets up the whole day well for a Saxmundham move. Open the curtains, switch on the lights so we can see clearly, and make sure nothing is still hidden in the loft, shed or behind a door. When we arrive we like a quick walk-round so you can point out the fragile boxes, the antiques and anything needing extra care, especially if you are moving heavier or older furniture into or out of one of Saxmundham's period homes with narrower stairs.

Leave out a kettle, mugs and a few biscuits for the team if you can; it keeps everyone going through a longer day and our crews always appreciate it. Keep children and pets in their safe room with a clear note on the door, because the front door will stand open for much of the morning and cats in particular will bolt given the chance. Tell us where they are so we all keep that door shut. Letting us get straight to loading means we make the most of the day and reach Saxmundham in good time for an unhurried unload.

Before we leave the old property, do a proper walk-through together. Check every cupboard, the loft, the shed, the garden and behind every door, because things get left in the rush, and a forgotten box is a real problem when there is a long drive between the two homes. Confirm the inventory, sort the handover of the old keys and make sure you have the keys for your new Saxmundham home. Then meet us at the other end, where we will unload to the right rooms and rebuild the beds so you have somewhere comfortable to sleep on your first night.

sections.h: Arriving in Saxmundham: The First Hours
sections.body:
Arriving at the new home is where all that labelling pays off. As we unload, we carry each box to the room marked on its side, so your home takes shape steadily rather than becoming one great pile in the hall. Point us to where the big furniture goes before we bring it in; placing a wardrobe correctly first time is far easier than shuffling it once it is full, and in older properties with awkward corners that bit of planning really counts. If you have colour-coded the doors, the whole unload moves faster and with fewer questions.

Get the kitchen working enough for a brew and a simple meal, and ask us to make up the beds before we go. After a long day and a long drive out to east Suffolk, the difference between a made-up bed and a mattress buried under boxes is the difference between a good first night in Saxmundham and a miserable one. Find your essentials box, put the kettle on, and resist the urge to unpack everything tonight. The boxes will still be there in the morning, and a proper rest is the best possible start in your new town.

Over the following days, unpack at a sensible pace, room by room, and take time to enjoy where you now live. A first trip out to the coast at Aldeburgh or Southwold, a walk through the gentle countryside around the town and a wander down the Saxmundham high street are how a new house starts to feel like home. Register with a local GP and dentist, sort your utility and council accounts, and let yourself settle. We will have done our part by then, and every load we carry is fully covered, so you can put your energy into enjoying life on the edge of the Suffolk Heritage Coast.

faqs: What should I plan first for a moving day in Saxmundham?

The distance and the access. Saxmundham is a good run out into east Suffolk, so timings matter, and an early start helps. Picture where a lorry can stop too, as some streets near the high street are narrow. Tell us the details early and we will plan the route, schedule and any shuttle vehicle needed.
Why does an early start help for a Saxmundham move?

Because the drive out to east Suffolk takes time, an early, steady start gives plenty of room for the journey and an unhurried unload at the other end, rather than a race against fading light in winter. Being fully packed and ready the night before is what makes that early start possible.
What goes in the essentials box for my Saxmundham move?

Pack the kettle, mugs, tea, milk and biscuits, phone chargers, basic toiletries, a change of clothes, any medication, important documents and a few toys for the children. Keep it in your own car so it is to hand the moment you reach your new Saxmundham home after the drive.
Can you move heavy or period furniture into an older Saxmundham home?

Yes. We regularly move heavy and period pieces into older market-town properties with narrower staircases and lower doorways. Point these items out on the morning walk-round so we can pad them and plan the carry carefully, and the move goes smoothly even in the trickier historic homes.
